nodes.functional_summary: Immutable 2D vector algebra primitive (encapsulated geometry). Pure math, no system logic. Operations return new records; never mutate operands.
Deterministic seedable PRNG (mulberry32). Generator state carried as an explicit value and threaded by callers; no hidden global entropy. Guarantees replayable design tooling and agent simulation.

Fixed-timestep accumulator. Banks variable real delta and emits a whole number of fixed simulation steps (FIXED_STEP_SECONDS = 1/60). Clamps to MAX_FRAME_SECONDS to prevent spiral-of-death. Pure value state.
Immutable root state tree GameState<TWorld> plus InputFrame. Core owns tick/clock/rng/input; gameplay-specific data lives in the abstract world slot so core is decoupled from systems and content.
The System<TWorld> contract: a pure step(state)=>state function for one fixed step. Systems are stateless between frames and never read another system's internals.

Single source of truth. Holds current + previous snapshots and is the only place the current snapshot is swapped (commit). Optional deep-freeze on commit catches accidental mutation in tests. Idempotent commit.

Deterministic Read->Process->Emit runtime loop. tick(realDeltaSeconds) reads the current snapshot from StateManager, calls Clock.advance() once to get a whole fixed-step count, runs the ordered System pipeline once per step (tick incremented by exactly 1 per step), and commits every resulting snapshot through StateManager. Commits even on a zero-step tick to persist the banked clock accumulator (dm-0007). Exposes interpolationAlpha for the renderer. Generic over TWorld; no gameplay assumptions.

Deterministic SDLC drift gate. Dependency-free Node runner that checks pkg-covers-src, pkg-not-stale, no-placeholders, handoff-fresh, backlog-integrity, stage-consistency (vs meta/active_task.json), and (manual) tests-green against live repo state. CLI + Stop-hook modes; fails open on internal error, loud on findings. Reads policy from tools/sdlc/sdlc.yaml.

SDLC Stage Manager. Tracks the active task slice through the nine-stage loop and persists it to meta/active_task.json (live) + meta/runs/ (archive) for mid-slice resumability. Enforces stage order; records acceptance-criteria state. CLI: start/advance/criterion/status/done/abort.

Declarative SDLC gate policy: enforcement level (warn|block) and per-gate severity/enabled/when overrides. Override-only over gate.js built-in defaults, so a malformed line degrades safely toward enforcement.

Branded stable EntityId type + RUNTIME_SPAWN_ID_PREFIX const. Pure data declarations. Authored ids minted only by the schema validator (S2.3); runtime ids from WorldState's deterministic spawn counter in the disjoint rt: namespace.
TransformDef: entity placement record (position Vec2 in world units, facing ±1). Logic-free plain data. Coordinate convention lives solely in docs/level_schema.md (S2.2).
AabbDef: axis-aligned collision volume record (halfExtents + offset Vec2s). Geometry data only; overlap/resolution logic belongs to P3 systems. Positivity enforced by the schema validator, not here.

BehaviorDef: closed discriminated union of the 12 PRD-§16 entity behavior payloads (environmental, hazards, interactive, kinetic modifiers) + EntityKind + ENTITY_KINDS iteration list (test-enforced exhaustive over the union). Time fields authored in seconds; dynamics-are-entities per dm-0009.
EntityDef: one authored entity as pure data {id, transform, collider, behavior}. behavior.kind is the single discriminant (no redundant kind field — mismatch is unrepresentable).

TilemapDef: flat row-major grid of closed tile-kind ids (TILE_KIND_BY_ID: 0 empty, 1 solid) + width/height/tileSize. Permanently-static geometry only (dm-0009); frozen and reference-shared at runtime. Coordinate convention normatively in docs/level_schema.md (dm-0015).

TriggerDef: standalone interconnection wiring records {id, source, targets, action, once} with TriggerId brand, closed TriggerActionKind union + TRIGGER_ACTIONS list + TRIGGER_ACTION_TARGET_KIND compatibility table (dm-0015). Decouples signal sources from effects (REQ-154).

GdosMetadata: PRD-§13 GDOS block — targetKgNode, difficultyVectors (closed provisional 4-axis set + DIFFICULTY_AXES list), emotionalBudgetCurve (§6 four-metric keyframes), creatorMomentFrame (minimal shape). Structural in P2, semantic ownership P5 (dm-0012, dm-0015).

LevelDefinition schema root: schemaVersion (LEVEL_SCHEMA_VERSION=1, hard-reject policy dm-0010), levelId+title, gdos, tilemap, entities, triggers, constraints (spawn/goal/parTimeTiersSeconds — axiom boundary dm-0011: no maxJumps ever). Pure data.

serializeLevel(): canonical JSON serializer — fixed key order via explicit per-record canonicalizers, two-space indent, -0 normalized, exhaustive behavior switch. Definition-time I/O in src/schema/ (dm-0013); never called from the engine loop. Canonical form anchored by S2.4 golden hash (dm-0014).

parseLevel(unknown)/parseLevelText(string): the ONLY construction path from untrusted input to LevelDefinition (parse-don't-validate). Never throws; collects ALL errors with JSON-pointer paths. Strict unknown-key rejection (dm-0014), finite-only numbers with -0 normalization (dm-0010), schemaVersion hard-reject, id uniqueness + rt: namespace guard, trigger referential integrity + kind-compatibility (dm-0015). Sanctioned minting point for EntityId/TriggerId brands.

Jump Once's concrete TWorld: WorldState {level (deep-frozen, reference-shared LevelDefinition — dm-0009), entities runtime records INDEX-ALIGNED with level.entities (S3.1 invariant systems pair def↔state by), player position/prevPosition/velocity/grounded/groundEntity (S3.6-7: prevPosition feeds the swept lethal check; groundEntity drives carry/ice/collapse), jumpLock (S3.5 — THE AXIOM's machine state), runState/attemptCount/spawnTick (S3.4 lifecycle), triggerActivePrev[]/triggerFired[] (S3.7 rising-edge + once tracking), nextSpawnSerial}. EntityState += activationTick|null, firstContactTick|null, collapsed (S3.6), doorOpen (S3.7 door runtime). + RunState + JumpPhase + JumpLockState + JumpOnceState alias. instantiateWorld(def): pure deterministic instantiation (freezes def, idempotent) — reload IS this function, so per-life state (incl. the S3.5 jump lock) resets by construction (dm-0018); createInitialState(def, seed): full GameState composition. The runtime-consumption evidence for REQ-120/121 (dm-0012).

TUNING — the single normative campaign-global physics/feel record (dm-0018): gravityY (+y down), runSpeed (instant accel/decel, S3.3), jumpSpeed + anticipationTicks (THE jump, S3.5), iceAccel (frictionless-ice ramp, S3.6), maxFallSpeed, playerHalfExtents. Pure data (logic-free scan applies); values are ledgered decisions, not tweaks (changes invalidate replays/goldens). Fields are added per P3 slice as they gain a consumer. Per-level overrides would be a schema version bump, deliberately not data here.

COLLISION_CLASS_BY_KIND — closed, total classification of every EntityKind as solid | lethal | sensor (P3 plan point 3; REQ-154 systems key on data classification, never entity identity). Runtime state refines (door open, floor collapsed, laser phase) but never overrides the static class. Totality asserted by unit test against ENTITY_KINDS.

playerPhysicsSystem + stepPlayerPhysics(world): deterministic fixed-step player body physics (S3.1, +S3.6 carry). Platform CARRY: last-tick ground entity's derived delta folded into the sweep (0 for tile/static). Semi-implicit (symplectic) Euler with terminal-fall clamp; swept, axis-separated resolution — X (carry+input merged) then Y in two phases (carry, then gravity so grounding stays robust under an upward platform) — clamping to the nearest solid face (tiles via range scan, solid entities via per-axis face test over the S3.2 quadtree candidate set), exact snapping, no tunneling at any speed (dm-0017). Records playerGroundEntity (the clamping entity index or -1); collapsed floors are non-solid; open doors are non-solid (runtime doorOpen, S3.7). Gravity is scaled by any gravityZone the player centre is inside (S3.8 kinetic modifier, dm-0021 — reads entity data, never jumpLock). Blocked axes zero their velocity. Reads state.world ONLY — never state.input (S3.3 owns input).

sensorsSystem (S3.7): after playerPhysics, before hazardsAndGoal. Signal sources (pressurePlate/proximityZone) are ACTIVE when the player strictly overlaps their AABB; triggers fire on the RISING EDGE (held plates don't re-fire), `once` triggers at most once/life, in AUTHORED ARRAY ORDER (deterministic cascades). Closed action union edits target runtime: open/close/toggleDoor -> doorOpen; collapseFloor -> firstContactTick; activatePlatform -> activationTick (wakes a dormant triggered mover). Trusts the validator's referential integrity (dm-0015). Pure; same snapshot when nothing fires.

entityKinematicsSystem (S3.6): SECOND in the canonical pipeline (after lifecycle, before playerControl). Resolves every tick-DERIVED entity runtime value once per step (dm-0016 closed-form, never incremental): mover world position = transform + arc-length point along the local-space waypoint polyline (looping wraps; linear/triggered ping-pong; auto modes elapse from world.spawnTick, triggered from activationTick or dormant); mover velocity = derived per-tick delta (for carry). Collapsing floors: latch firstContactTick from last tick's grounding, derive collapsed = elapsed >= collapseDelaySeconds (one-way per life). Arc-length arithmetic only (no trig, dm-0017). Pure; same snapshot when nothing is dynamic.

lifecycleSystem (S3.4): FIRST in the canonical pipeline. Consumes 'defeated' or resetPressed (its share of the by-field input split, dm-0019 refined) into a fresh life: reload = instantiateWorld(world.level) — pure re-instantiation, frozen level reference-shared through it; only attemptCount(+1) and spawnTick(=tick) survive the life boundary. One-tick defeat->reload latency = REQ-003 instant iteration. Per-life state (incl. S3.5 jump lock) resets by construction.

hazardsAndGoalSystem (S3.4 + S3.7 lethal): LAST in the canonical pipeline — judges the post-physics position. LETHAL HAZARDS (S3.7): spike/movingHazard always lethal, laser lethal only on-phase (pure fn of tick); a SWEPT test (player centre segment playerPrevPosition->playerPosition vs Minkowski-enlarged hazard AABB grown by the hazard's per-tick motion; slab method, arithmetic only) -> 'defeated', so a fast player cannot skip a thin hazard. Goal strict-AABB overlap vs constraints.goal -> 'completed' (freezes world until reset); player fully below the tilemap bottom edge -> 'defeated'. Defeat (hazard or fall-out) checked before goal.

playerControlSystem (S3.3/5/6 + S3.8 spring/conveyor): consumer of moveAxis and jumpPressed under the by-field input split (dm-0019; Lifecycle owns resetPressed). Surface-based base velocity: instant accel/decel off ice (REQ-150); frictionless-ice momentum ramp (S3.6); grounded-on-conveyor adds surfaceVelocityX; grounded-on-spring sets velocity = launchVelocity (S3.8 kinetic modifiers, per-entity data, dm-0021 — NONE touch jumpLock). Drives THE AXIOM's single-jump lock machine (dm-0020): grounded press -> anticipating(TUNING.anticipationTicks) -> ONE impulse vy=-TUNING.jumpSpeed (overrides any spring vy — axiom precedence) -> spent forever until reload re-instantiates (dm-0018); air presses ignored-not-consumed; no coyote/no buffering. Runs BEFORE playerPhysicsSystem. Pure.

Deterministic quadtree over entity AABBs (S3.2, REQ-162 P3 share). buildEntityQuadtree(world): pure — array insertion order, fixed capacity 8 / max depth 8, exact-midpoint splits, straddlers stay at the parent, root = tilemap world rect expanded to admit out-of-rect entities. queryQuadtree(tree, box): ascending entity indices under the SAME strict-overlap convention as the physics sweep (flush != overlap) — result set exactly equals brute force (fuzz-proven). Derived data, rebuilt per consumer step, never stored in state.
SessionStart hook. Deterministically injects the Reboot-Lifecycle reminder plus a live summary of meta/handoff_latest.json into context at every session start. Fails soft if the handoff is missing.

ReplayTape format + canonical serializer/strict parser (dm-0023; ownership assigned at P4 planning per P2 open question #5). A tape = {schemaVersion, levelId, seed(uint32), frames: InputFrame[]} — the full reproduction recipe for a deterministic run. dm-0010/dm-0014 discipline verbatim: fixed key order, strict unknown-key rejection, Result-typed path-qualified errors, never throws, byte-idempotent round-trip. Consumed by P4 harness/solvers; P8 input recording and P9 saves adopt this format, never redefine it.

The pure agent decision contract (dm-0024): decide(state, memory) -> {input, memory}. All agent statefulness threaded through AgentMemory (held intent, countdowns, per-life plan, agent RNG cursor); agents draw ONLY from their own seeded stream, never state.rng, so tape replays reproduce live runs bit for bit. createAgentMemory(agentSeed) builds the initial memory. First module of src/eval/ (dm-0022: one-way dependency — the sim never imports eval).

The five REQ-141 archetypes (firstTime/cautious/experienced/expertSpeedrunner/curiousExplorer) as frozen ArchetypeParams data records over ONE shared reactive decision core (dm-0024: data-parameterized policies, not code forks): walk toward goal, sense walls/gaps/hazards in tile space, commit THE jump with archetype hesitation/lookahead, caution pauses (rising-edge), exploration prefix. Jump availability sensed from world.jumpLock.phase — the axiom is engine truth, never re-modeled (dm-0020). Whitelist math only (dm-0017 extended to src/eval/, scan-tested).

Headless run + replay: runAgent(def, seed, policy, budget) drives the sim via CANONICAL_PIPELINE (normative order: lifecycle -> entityKinematics -> playerControl -> playerPhysics -> sensors -> hazardsAndGoal, dm-0021), records one InputFrame per tick into a ReplayTape, and halts on completion or data-driven EvalBudget (maxTicks/maxAttempts) with typed outcome (completed|timeout) — halting is a property. replayTape(def, seed, frames) re-drives with no agent; bit-equality with the live final state is the determinism proof. replayObserved(def, seed, frames)->TickObservation[] (S5.1) re-drives collecting per-tick runState + player position + lethal-class entity positions, feeding the P5 evidence assembler's death extraction + REQ-016 fairness snapshot. Agent stream seed derived via fixed additive offset (agentStreamSeed), separate from sim RNG (dm-0024).

Bounded deterministic reachability search (dm-0025): macro-action BFS (hold one InputFrame holdTicks steps; 6 fixed actions moveAxis×jumpPressed) over the frozen sim, quantized-signature dedup (sub-tile lattice + jumpLock phase + persistent entity flags), death/goal as terminal leaves (one-life space, dm-0018). searchReachability(def,seed,opts)->{nodes,goalIndex,exhausted,truncated}; reconstructFrames walks parent pointers to a witness. THE AXIOM is engine truth: jumpLock.phase in the signature makes 'available' vs 'spent' distinct states; never counts/caps jumps. Whitelist math; always halts (node cap + visited set). S4.4: gained an optional `forbidden` predicate — any macro whose swept path touches a forbidden state is tainted and pruned (used by exploit filtration for skip-constrained reachability).

Exactly-one-jump solvability audit (REQ-141). Two tiers (P4 plan finding 7): fast path runs the five archetypes (any completion => solvable + its tape as witness); fallback runs the bounded search (goal leaf => solvable + reconstructed witness; exhausted frontier => 'unsolvable'; truncated => 'inconclusive', never a false unsolvable). auditSolvability(def,opts)->SolvabilityVerdict{classification,method,witness?,nodesExplored}; witnessCompletes replays the tape to self-prove. Axiom is ground truth read from the sim; a level needing >1 jump is unsolvable by construction (dm-0011/dm-0020).

Softlock (dead-zone) detection (REQ-141, dm-0026). Runs the S4.2 search exhaustively (stopAtGoal=false), then two backward-reachability sweeps over the successor graph: goalReachable and deathReachable. A trapped node = reachable non-terminal in NEITHER set (can't win, can't die — dying reloads to a fresh life, so death is an escape, not a softlock). detectSoftlock(def,opts)->SoftlockVerdict{hasSoftlock,trappedRegions,deepestTrapped,trappedCount,witness?,exhaustive}. Sound 'clean' only when exhaustive; witness drives the player into the trap. Whitelist math.

Exploit filtration (REQ-141, dm-0027). Challenge envelope = union of corridor lethal-hazard AABBs (on the spawn->goal box, expanded by corridorMargin) padded by envelopeMargin, tracking live entity positions. auditExploit(def,opts)->ExploitVerdict{hasExploit,applicable,bypassedHazardIds,skipWitness?}: runs the S4.2 search with forbidden=envelope; a goal reachable while avoiding the envelope is an exploit (the authored danger is fully bypassable) with the skip tape as cheese; every-completion-engages => clean. Whitelist math.

Optimization-space model (REQ-101/102, dm-0028). computeOptimizationWindow(def,opts)->OptimizationVerdict{applicable,tiers,deltaSeconds,rejected,parPlausible,completions}. Runs the five archetypes; tiers anchored to the spread — Discovery=First-Time clear, WorldRecord=Expert-Speedrunner clear, Good/Fast/Expert interpolated at 1/4,1/2,3/4; Curious-Explorer excluded from anchoring. delta=Discovery-WR seconds; reject when delta<minDeltaSeconds (default 0.25) — REQ-102 flat-layout rejection. Cross-checks authored parTimeTiersSeconds: parPlausible=false when authored optimal par < sim WR. Times via FIXED_STEP_SECONDS; whitelist math. dm-0030: the World-Record anchor is now the FASTEST known route — min of the Expert-Speedrunner clear and the bounded search optimal route (searchReachability) — so the delta reflects genuine route optimization, not just reaction time. Reports worldRecordSource + searchOptimalSeconds.

Macro Curriculum Validation — the SECOND (macro) validation pass (REQ-140/142, dm-0029). PURE data logic over an ordered CurriculumLevel[] (the minimal chapter contract: per-level local verdicts + difficulty + required/introduced mechanics + curiosity); runs NO simulation and imports nothing from the sim or the local pass (isolation is import-enforced). validateCurriculum(chapter,opts)->MacroVerdict with the four §15 criteria: Cognitive Structural Mapping (no orphan mechanic requirement; bounded new concepts/level), Cross-Chapter Degradation (no difficulty spike or end-degradation), Curiosity Progression (stays above floor; not monotonically dead), Graduation Assessment (finale is a capstone combining prior mechanics), plus a chapter-health precondition (all solvable, no softlock/exploit). Whitelist math.

Pure data records the GDOS scoring engine emits (P5/S5.1, dm-0031/0032). DesignDecision (source/subject/verdict/summary/findings + optional five Intent-Repository fields — REQ-111), MetricScore (metric/score/threshold/pass), GateResult (a gate's scores+pass+findings+decisions), GdosReport (a level's gates aggregated, stamped with profileId). Trivial constructors metricScore/gateResult/gdosReport derive pass from >= threshold and all-metrics-pass. No sim/audit imports; no arithmetic.

ScoringProfile — the versioned data record holding EVERY calibration constant (dm-0031, the load-bearing P5 decision): emotional thresholds (REQ-055), streamability thresholds (REQ-056), IDS viewport+min/max+fairness (REQ-061), novelty distance weights (REQ-053, added S5.5 within v1 — no persisted profiles existed), and all estimator coefficients. Gate logic contains ZERO calibration literals; recalibration is a data change (the CDRE channel, S5.8). DEFAULT_PROFILE freezes the PRD's own numbers. parseProfile/parseProfileText mirror the level-parser discipline (never throw, path-qualified Result errors, strict keys, finite numbers, hard version reject, max>=min, non-zero weights). Imports only the SchemaError type from schema/parse.

EvidenceBundle — everything a level is judged from, assembled ONCE (dm-0031): def + archetype ArchetypeRun[] + observed DeathEvent[] (with lookback snapshots for the REQ-016 fairness check) + the four P4 local verdicts. assembleEvidence is a PURE combiner (freezes the parts; runs no audits). Imports the four verdict types + ReplayTape + ArchetypeName + Vec2/EntityKind/LevelDefinition as TYPES ONLY (import type), so gdos gains no runtime dependency on the local audit pass — the scan-enforced no-re-auditing discipline. (S5.9 subtractive pass removed the unused runFor accessor.)
Shared scoring primitive: clamp100(x)=max(0,min(100,x)). The [0,100] scale is the DEFINITION of a GDOS score (not calibration, dm-0031), so its endpoints are not magic numbers. Whitelist math only.

The Mechanic x Environment x Emotion x Optimization-Style x Player-Type design-space matrix + coverage tracker (REQ-040/041, dm-0034). Axes are DERIVED from registries so they can't drift: MECHANIC_AXIS=ENTITY_KINDS, PLAYER_TYPE_AXIS=Object.keys(ARCHETYPES), ENVIRONMENT=modifier kinds+baseline, EMOTION_ARC=the REQ-015 six phases, OPTIMIZATION_STYLE=the five tiers. Also exports KINETIC_KINDS (spring/gravityZone/conveyor/movers — the shared kinetic grouping consumed by the streamability gate and the emergent-fun probe). extractCoverage(bundle)->exercised cell keys; coverageMatrix(bundles[])->union + per-axis marginals. Whitelist math; imports registries as data + the EvidenceBundle type.

Economy of Mechanics metric (REQ-042, dm-0034): economy = distinct covered cells (Depth) / mechanic count, over a CoverageMatrix; per-mechanic depth = distinct cells that mechanic participates in. preferByEconomy(deepen,addMechanic) encodes 'exhaust variations before adding a mechanic' (higher economy wins; ties favor deepening); deepestMechanic finds the one to keep exhausting. Whitelist math; imports the CoverageMatrix + EntityKind types.

Emotional-threshold gate (REQ-055; REQ-015 arc semantics; dm-0031/0036). Honest DELIVERED proxies over the bundle, all coefficients from the profile: Confidence~First-Time forgiveness (100-reloads*penalty), Curiosity~Curious-Explorer route overshoot*gain, Surprise~plan invalidation (max reloads before an adapted clear)*gain, Mastery~optimization delta/reference. Authored emotional-budget curve is the intent cross-check (delivered-vs-intended as findings). scoreEmotional->GateResult; computeEmotionalScores reused by the streamability gate. Whitelist math; pure.

Streamability-matrix gate (REQ-056, dm-0031). Proxies over the bundle, coefficients from the profile: Reaction Density~(deaths+hazards+kinetic)/clear-duration vs reference, Clip Potential~delivered Surprise (reused from the emotional gate) + kinetic count, Replay Value~optimization delta + distinct clear times (route multiplicity), Shareability~profile-weighted composite of the three. scoreStreamability->GateResult. Whitelist math; pure.

Information Density regulator + failure-visibility fairness gate (REQ-061; REQ-016 P5 share; dm-0035). The 'screen' is the profile viewport slid over the level counting info elements (entities+goal) per window; over max=overwhelm, global peak under min=boring. The same window is the REQ-016 primitive: a death is unfair iff its killer was outside the viewport around the player lookbackTicks before impact; falls (no attributed killer) are fair. scoreInfoDensity->GateResult (metric pass flags encode band/limit tests directly). Whitelist math; pure.

judgeLevel(evidence,profile)->GdosReport — the single seam the P10 pipeline reads (dm-0031). Pure over the bundle; runs the emotional (S5.2), streamability (S5.3), and info-density (S5.4) gates and aggregates their results. S5.5-S5.8 gates/curation append here. Whitelist math (none).

Novelty search metric (REQ-053, dm-0038 — resolves P5 open question 4). buildDescriptor(bundle) -> fixed-length NoveltyDescriptor: mechanic histogram (ENTITY_KINDS-order fractions) + geometry signature (4x4 solid-fraction bands) + trajectory shape (8 bucketed moveAxis means + first-jump position + hasJump/hasTrajectory). descriptorDistance(a,b,profile) = profile-weighted mean of per-component normalized Euclidean distances (weights = profile.novelty, dm-0031). noveltyDivergence(candidate, corpus[], profile) = distance to the NEAREST corpus member; null on empty corpus (content gate: no global registry exists). PURE gdos module (dm-0037): no sim, no search. Whitelist math (sqrt/min/max/floor).

Emergent-fun discovery probe (REQ-054). probeEmergentFun(def, opts) walks the bounded S4.2 reachability graph (stopAtGoal false) and flags KINETIC ANCHORS: reachable states whose velocity escapes the plain-movement envelope (|vx| > runSpeed*factor, or upward > jumpSpeed*factor; downward never flagged — falling is gravity, not emergence). Each anchor is deduped by quantized position+axis, attributed to the nearest KINETIC_KINDS entity's live position within a radius, and carries reconstructFrames(...) input frames so P7 can reproduce and design around the moment. Thresholds are factors over TUNING (data, options record). Lives at TOP-LEVEL src/eval/ per dm-0037 (it EXECUTES a search — never a gdos/ module). Deterministic; whitelist math.

The executable GDOS Design Memory / Intent Repository (REQ-050/051/111, dm-0032/dm-0040) — the pure text->records->text layer over meta/design_memory_ledger.json (that file's own header designates it the backing store). parseDesignMemory: strict parse, path-qualified Result errors, closed ACCEPTED|REJECTED set, dm-#### ids unique, YYYY-MM-DD dates, five non-empty Intent Repository fields per entry; rejected_ideas[] stays empty (rejected ideas = decisions with status REJECTED). v1.1 (P7/S7.1, dm-0055) unlocked the mechanic_lifecycle_registry: typed MechanicLifecycleEntry records (mechanic = closed EntityKind, unique; one of the nine REQ-082 LIFECYCLE_STAGES; prune|convert disposition exactly when Retired; forward-only contiguous history chained from Introduction to the current stage, dated + evidence-backed) parsed/serialized canonically; setMechanicEntry = the validated PURE upsert. The stage TRACKER lives in gen/lifecycle. findPriorArt = the REQ-051 parse-before-proposing term search. appendDecision is PURE (new document; date is a parameter — no Date.now) with an exact-title repetition guard; nextDecisionId mints max+1. serializeDesignMemory = canonical bytes (fixed key order, 2-space, trailing newline), byte-idempotent against the live file. fs stays at callers. Dogfooded: dm-0040 was appended to the live ledger via this store.

The Creative-Director machinery (REQ-020/021/022; REQ-012 curation share; dm-0041). killSwitch(bundle, report, context): kills on (1) failed GDOS gates, (2) a completion whose tape never presses THE jump — the REQ-012 'does not test the constraint' evidence proxy, (3) addsNewMechanic while the REQ-042 economy comparison favors deepening; every kill carries the full five Intent Repository fields (a design-intent commitment for the S5.6 store). firstPartyReview(subject, attestations, report?): the three Section-3 criteria (Self-Explanation, Hours of Interest, Inevitable Polish) as HONEST typed attestations with mandatory rationales — an empty rationale is itself a failure; deterministic verdict logic, never faked measurements (finding 10). subtractivePass(items, answers): the six pruning questions as data (SUBTRACTIVE_QUESTIONS); any NO = removal candidate, any missing answer = incomplete (silence is not a keep). PURE (dm-0037): decision procedures over computed data only.

The Continuous Design Research Engine (REQ-052, dm-0033/dm-0042): a DETERMINISTIC profile-evolution loop, not ML and not self-modifying code. mine(inputs, options) -> CdreProposal[] observes but never mutates, emitting four kinds in fixed order: threshold-adjustment (a >=-cutoff metric failing >= failureRateForLowering of reports proposes a LOWER bar at the configured percentile; a metric passing everywhere by >= raiseMarginPoints proposes a RAISE — the symmetric signal that prevents a Goodhart ratchet), estimator-unmeasurable (a metric scoring 0 in every report is an evidence-source problem, never a threshold one), coverage-gap (unexercised design-space axis values, REQ-041), recurring-rejection (a kill reason dominating the decision record is systemic, not calibration). Scores gathered under differing thresholds are skipped (cross-profile scores are incomparable, dm-0031); info-density metrics are never threshold-mined (band/limit tests). Every proposal carries the five Intent Repository fields and comes back PROPOSED. apply(profile, proposal) accepts ONLY an ACCEPTED threshold-adjustment, is pure, versions profileId (never the schema version), and round-trips the result through parseProfile so an evolved profile is valid by construction. PURE gdos module (dm-0037): no sim/search/IO/clock. (S5.9 subtractive pass removed the unused CDRE_BASE_PROFILE alias.)

The end-to-end P5 seam (S5.1). evaluateLevel/assembleLevelEvidence run the four P4 audits + five archetypes ONCE, observe deaths (replayObserved) with killer attribution + lookback for the REQ-016 fairness check, assemble the EvidenceBundle, and judgeLevel. This is the ONLY module that both runs the audits AND touches gdos, which is why it lives at the top of src/eval/ and NOT under gdos/ — keeping gdos free of audit-function imports (dm-0031, scan-enforced). Deterministic (shared run seed; squared-distance attribution); whitelist math. (S5.9 subtractive pass removed the unused TICK_SECONDS re-export of FIXED_STEP_SECONDS.)

S6.1 — the versioned CampaignProfile calibration record for P6 Campaign Intelligence (REQ-030/031/032, dm-0045). Deliberately a SEPARATE schema from gdos/Profile.ts ScoringProfile, never an in-place extension of it (dm-0039 grace spent; the two concerns -- per-level GDOS scoring vs. campaign-level trajectory -- must not compete for one versioned document). Groups: BehaviorProfile (S6.2 TapeAnalyzer frame-count sensitivity), MasteryProfile (S6.3 MechanicTracker confidence/routing thresholds), ChapterHealthProfile (S6.4/S6.5 EMA baseline decay + spike drop threshold), RetentionWeights (S6.5 retention composite, not all zero), trendWindowLevels. parseCampaignProfile mirrors the ScoringProfile strict-parse discipline exactly (dm-0010/0014): never throws, path-qualified errors, strict unknown-key rejection, finite numbers, hard schemaVersion reject. DEFAULT_CAMPAIGN_PROFILE is the S6.1 calibration baseline pending future ledgered recalibration. S6.3 (dm-0050): MasteryProfile gained knowledgeLearningRate (EMA rate for KnowledgeModel); masteryRoutingConfidenceThreshold precisely redefined as a coverage-cell top-tier ratio threshold. S6.4 (dm-0051): ChapterHealthProfile gained trendFlatTolerance (score-point band for rising/falling/flat classification). S6.5 (dm-0052): gained top-level trendMagnitudeTolerance ([0,1]-scale skillCurve/curiosityTrend classification, distinct from chapterHealthCalibration.trendFlatTolerance's 0-100 chapter-score scale); trendWindowLevels (shipped unused at S6.1) is now genuinely consumed via the 2/(N+1) EMA-rate conversion.

S6.1 — the CampaignState kernel: types all ten REQ-031 macro variables (knowledgeState, behaviorState, emotionalState, skillCurve, mechanicsIntroduced, mechanicsMastered, optimizationDepth, curiosityTrend, chapterHealth, retentionPrediction) plus ZERO_CAMPAIGN_STATE, the additive identity (dm-0043 -- CampaignDirector, S6.5, updates state via a PURE fold, never mutates in place). Also defines BehaviorSignals (REQ-032 Player Behavior Model -- every field frame-derived, dm-0043; P8-independent per dm-0044), EmotionalState, TrendReading, CampaignAlert (evidentiary, never a bare boolean, dm-0048), ChapterHealthReport (REQ-142 P6 share -- mirrors MacroVerdict field-for-field, dm-0046), LevelRecord (the P6 input unit: GdosReport + ReplayTape + MacroVerdict, all P4/P5 output consumed as data), and CampaignReport (S6.5 CampaignDirector output). Imports GdosReport/ArchetypeRun/MacroVerdict with `import type` ONLY -- zero runtime coupling to gdos/, the sim, or AgentHarness (dm-0047: campaign/ never re-audits, mirroring gdos/'s own no-re-auditing rule one abstraction level up). S6.3 (dm-0050): mechanicsIntroduced/mechanicsMastered widened to ReadonlySet<EntityKind>; LevelRecord gained mechanicsExercised: ReadonlySet<EntityKind> (caller-supplied per-level mechanic set). S6.5: TrendReading.magnitude precisely defined as the current EMA-rolled value of the underlying signal (not a raw step delta) -- direction reports movement vs the previous magnitude.

S6.2 -- analyzeTape(run: ArchetypeRun, profile: CampaignProfile) -> BehaviorSignals (REQ-032, dm-0043/dm-0044/dm-0049). Every signal is frame-derived from run.tape.frames except retryCount (run.attempts) and dropOffRate (run.outcome === "timeout" ? 1 : 0), read directly from the P4 harness output rather than re-derived. hesitationFrames: longest no-input gap before a jump-press edge that clears profile.behavior.hesitationFrameThreshold (brief reaction-time pauses do not count). commitmentSpeed: frame index of the first jump-press edge, undefined if none. panicBurstCount: greedy non-overlapping count of windows (profile.behavior.panicBurstWindowFrames) containing >= profile.behavior.panicBurstInputCount active-input edges (jump/reset/movement-direction-change edges, not held state). platformCheckCount: a moveAxis direction reversal with no jump-press within profile.behavior.platformCheckWindowFrames frames afterward -- an input-pattern proxy for ledge-checking that needs no level geometry (dm-0049, replacing the withdrawn geometry-based S6.1 design). Depends only on ArchetypeRun + CampaignProfile -- zero coupling to LevelDefinition, AgentHarness, or P8 telemetry (dm-0044).

S6.3 -- trackMechanics(coverageMatrix: CoverageMatrix, knowledgeState, profile: CampaignProfile) -> { mechanicsIntroduced, mechanicsMastered } (REQ-031, REQ-041 P6 share, dm-0045/dm-0050). CoverageMatrix is supplied by the CALLER (the cumulative matrix over every level processed so far) -- this module never imports or calls DesignSpace.coverageMatrix() itself (dm-0045, one source of truth). mechanicsIntroduced = coverageMatrix.mechanicsCovered verbatim. mechanicsMastered requires BOTH knowledgeState[mechanic] >= profile.mastery.masteryConfidenceThreshold AND a top-tier coverage ratio (of the mechanic's distinct exercised CoverageMatrix cells, the fraction at the 'worldRecord' optimization tier) >= profile.mastery.masteryRoutingConfidenceThreshold -- replacing an unbuildable archetype-identity-based routing-confidence design (dm-0050: LevelRecord carries only one ArchetypeRun, no reliable archetype identity to anchor an exploration-archetype check).

S6.3 -- updateKnowledge(knowledgeState, record: LevelRecord, profile: CampaignProfile) -> knowledgeState' (REQ-031 knowledgeState macro variable, dm-0050). Pure EMA: every mechanic in record.mechanicsExercised moves toward 1 if the level both passed its GDOS gates AND completed (not timeout), else toward 0, at profile.mastery.knowledgeLearningRate -- the same EMA technique as ChapterHealthProfile's rolling baseline (dm-0048) applied one layer down. Unrelated knowledgeState keys carry over unchanged. Never mutates its input (dm-0043).

S6.4 -- aggregateChapterHealth(macroVerdict: MacroVerdict, priorChapterHealth: ChapterHealthReport | undefined, profile: CampaignProfile) -> ChapterHealthReport (REQ-142 P6 share, dm-0046/dm-0051). The four macro criteria (cognitiveStructuralMapping, crossChapterDegradation, curiosityProgression, graduationAssessment) pass through from MacroVerdict verbatim -- P4 already computed them for this chapter, this module never re-derives them (dm-0046). score is a STRUCTURAL pass-count over MacroVerdict's five booleans (chapterHealthy + 4 criteria) out of 100, needing no calibration weight since the aggregation is a fact of MacroVerdict's own shape, not a tunable choice. trend compares this chapter's score against the prior chapter's under profile.chapterHealthCalibration.trendFlatTolerance. alerts is always [] -- spike detection needs the rolling EMA baseline across the WHOLE campaign, which only CampaignDirector (S6.5) accumulates during its fold; ChapterHealth reports one chapter's own score+trend only.

S6.5 -- the Game Director's fold (REQ-030 core, dm-0043/dm-0052). updateState(state, record, coverageMatrix, profile) -> CampaignState is the pure per-LEVEL fold step: behaviorState/dropOffRate roll via EMA, knowledgeState via KnowledgeModel, mechanicsIntroduced/Mastered via MechanicTracker, optimizationDepth from the cumulative CoverageMatrix's highest touched OPTIMIZATION_STYLE_AXIS tier (normalized), emotionalState EMA-rolled from GDOS's OWN per-level 'emotional-threshold' gate scores (never re-derived from behavior), skillCurve/curiosityTrend step-over-step EMA trends (TrendReading.magnitude = current rolled value, direction = movement vs previous magnitude, tolerance from profile.trendMagnitudeTolerance) with the EMA rate derived from trendWindowLevels via the standard 2/(N+1) conversion. processCampaign(chapters: ChapterRecord[], profile) -> CampaignReport folds a chapter-grouped campaign: each chapter's levels fold via updateState, then ChapterHealth.aggregateChapterHealth elevates its MacroVerdict, a difficulty spike is flagged via an EMA baseline over ChapterHealthReport.score COMPARED AGAINST THE BASELINE BEFORE this chapter's own score joins it (avoiding self-masking), and retentionPrediction recomputes as profile.retentionWeights' weighted average of five [0,1] components (inverse drop-off, optimizationDepth, inverse panic bursts, curiosity-trend direction, mean chapter health). Deterministic: proven by a same-input-twice test and a synthetic 3-chapter fixture campaign with a planted spike at chapter 2 (the IRD P6 exit condition).

S7.1 — GenProfile: the THIRD versioned calibration schema (dm-0057, extending the dm-0045 separation logic past ScoringProfile and CampaignProfile): every P7 generation threshold/weight/budget lives here, strict-parsed with the full P2 discipline (unknown keys rejected at every object, finite-number bounds, hard version pin GEN_PROFILE_SCHEMA_VERSION=1, path-qualified Result errors, never throws), DEFAULT_GEN_PROFILE frozen. v1 carries the lifecycle calibration (developmentMinLevels, saturationNoveltyThreshold, exhaustionConsecutiveLowNovelty); later P7 slices grow it per-concern exactly as CampaignProfile grew in P6. Zero calibration literals in gen logic; two-profile tests prove externalization. S7.3 grew the pda section (maxOpportunities cap, weakChapterHealthScore bar). S7.4 grew the generator section (corridor envelope, air rows, gap ceiling, par tiers, and the per-kind entityTuning dynamics — every gameplay value the template writes). S7.5 grew the creativity section (loop budget, combineProbability, diminishing-returns epsilon, and selection weights). S7.6 grew the intent section (minWords/maxWords band for the single-sentence gate). S7.7 grew the pipeline section (revisionBudget).

S7.1 — the REQ-082 mechanic lifecycle TRACKER (dm-0055). The registry SCHEMA lives in the Design Memory store (ledger v1.1); this module is the pure stage logic over those records, split CDRE-style (dm-0033) into observation and act: assessStage(entry, evidence, profile) climbs the nine-stage ladder (Introduction→Isolation→Development→Combination→Subversion→Mastery→Saturation→Exhaustion→Retirement) contiguously from caller-supplied observable evidence (usage counts, isolation/combination/subversion level existence, campaign mastery, novelty divergences) — advisory, never recommends Retirement; advanceStage is the explicit validated act (forward-only, dated, evidence-backed, prune|convert disposition exactly when Retiring); isBlocked = the REQ-082 reuse block (Exhaustion/Retirement; absent = fresh). All thresholds from GenProfile (dm-0057); pure, no clock, whitelist math.

S7.2 — the REQ-012 jump-relevance audit (dm-0041 deferred from P5; design dm-0056): auditJumpRelevance runs the S4.3 bounded reachability search with the S4.4 forbidden hook tainting any state whose jumpLock left its spawn phase (available — the forward-only available→anticipating→spent machine), so the BFS explores exactly the no-jump reachable set with ZERO Search.ts changes. Goal reached ⇒ jump-irrelevant (REQ-012 kill) with a no-jump witness tape, self-provable via witnessCompletesWithoutJump (replay completes with the lock still available); frontier exhausted ⇒ jump-necessary (the pass); node cap ⇒ inconclusive, honestly. Orthogonal to solvability — consumers compose solvable AND jump-necessary (the S7.7 pipeline Kinetic Simulation phase does). The axiom stays ground truth: reads world.jumpLock as fact, never re-implements it (dm-0020).

S7.3+S7.4 — the level-design vocabulary (dm-0059): LEVEL_ARCHETYPES, the closed twelve-value list from /level-design-principle (LEVEL archetypes = one-jump decision shapes, distinct from eval/Archetypes.ts player archetypes), plus LevelConcept — the pipeline phase-1 record typing the skill workflow answers (archetype, intentSentence [the REQ-091 subject — lives HERE, not in LevelDefinition], oneJumpDecision, mechanics, difficultyTarget, emotionalPhase, targetKgNode) — and validateConcept: structural gate (closed vocabularies, [0,1] difficulty bounds, mechanic uniqueness) including the REQ-082 lifecycle block via gen/lifecycle isBlocked. Intent RIGOR is the S7.6 gate, not phase 1.

S7.3 — the Procedural Design Assistant (REQ-060, dm-0058): discoverOpportunities(inputs, profile) is a deterministic data-fusion pass over five consumed-by-reference, optional-degrading signals — CoverageMatrix (CONCEPTUAL pair gaps: individually covered mechanic x emotion / mechanic x optimization-style values that never met — one level finer than CDRE axis mining), CampaignReport (SYSTEMIC: alerts first, then chapters under the profiled weak-health bar), labeled EmergentFunReports (STRUCTURAL: kinetic anchors, REQ-054 applied), CdreProposals (coverage-gap/recurring-rejection echoes, ranked last, REJECTED skipped) — with two mandatory FILTERS: gen/lifecycle isBlocked (REQ-082) and rejected-prior-art via findPriorArt (REQ-051). Emits ranked typed DesignOpportunity records (kind, suggested archetypes from a frozen /level-design-principle mapping, mechanics, rationale, sourceSignals provenance); the cap and weak-chapter bar live in GenProfile.pda; every drop is counted (blockedMechanic/rejectedPriorArt/overCap) — no silent caps. Never raw geometry.

S7.4 — the candidate generator (REQ-090 phases 1-2 substrate, REQ-081 operators; dm-0059/dm-0061): generateCandidate(concept, seed, profile) draws GenParams (corridorLength, gapWidth from executionPrecision, gapStart) inside a profiled envelope auto-widened to fit the concept mechanic count, builds the gap-corridor skeleton (approach / spiked pit / landing / goal — the proven-jumpable P4 fixture shape), places all twelve EntityKinds via a frozen per-kind slot table (spike = the pit; laser/movingHazard at the gap; spring/door landing; surfaces/plates/zones/movers approach; plate+door trigger-wired per dm-0015), writes GdosMetadata from the concept (difficulty vectors, per-phase emotional-curve presets, creator moment from the one-jump decision), and PROVES every emission via the P2 strict parser. mutateCandidate jitters one clamped parameter; combineCandidates coin-flips parameters from two parents (concept from parent a); both re-build and re-prove. Pure function of (concept, seed, profile) — threaded core/Rng, no clock; unbuildable concepts refused with typed SchemaError reasons.

S7.5 — the REQ-081 creativity & iteration evolutionary loop (dm-0062; REQ-053 applied): evolveLevel(concept, corpus, seed, genProfile, evalOptions) folds over generations — generate the seed candidate (gen/Generator), then each generation breeds variationsPerGeneration variations of the top-two survivors (mutateCandidate the best, or combineCandidates the top two into a hybrid at combineProbability), evaluates each through the public P5 seam (evaluateLevel), compares to memory via noveltyDivergence against the prior corpus, and selects by a profile-weighted composite of gate pass + mean gate score + clamped novelty. Survivors carry forward so the best score is monotone non-decreasing; halts when improvement < diminishingReturnsEpsilon (converged) or at hardCapGenerations. Pure function of its inputs (threaded core/Rng, deterministic eval seed); an unbuildable seed concept fails honestly with the generator typed reason. Calibration split (dm-0057): loop budget/operator-mix/selection-weights in GenProfile.creativity, gate scoring in the ScoringProfile inside evalOptions.

S7.6 — the REQ-091 Single-Sentence Intent Verification gate (dm-0063): verifyIntent(concept, profile) judges the concept intentSentence STRUCTURALLY (not NLP) — exactly one sentence (one terminal .!? ending the string), inside GenProfile.intent minWords..maxWords, and containing at least one causal connective from the closed LESSON_CONNECTIVES grammar (the label→lesson marker). Returns a typed IntentVerdict listing every failed reason (empty|not-one-sentence|too-short|too-long|no-lesson; empty short-circuits) with path-qualified findings. This is the pipeline Sign-off/Intent phase (S7.7 phase 7); a denial means the pipeline persists only the rejection, never a product. Pure over (concept, profile), whitelist math, no clock.

S7.7 — the REQ-090 eight-phase manufacturing pipeline (dm-0060/dm-0064; REQ-061 applied): manufactureLevel(concept, options, profile) drives a concept through Concept (validateConcept lifecycle blocking) → Structural Prototyping (generateCandidate, parse-proven) → Kinetic Simulation (solvable + jump-necessary [S7.2 counterfactual] + softlock-free; the entity-exploit audit is RECORDED as evidence, NOT a hard gate — it provably misfires on every one-jump pit incl. ONE_GAP, dm-0064) → AI Council (evaluateLevel → GdosReport.pass; the REQ-061 info-density regulation point) → Targeted Revision (bounded mutateCandidate retries, profile.pipeline.revisionBudget) → Optimization Layering (computeOptimizationWindow flat-layout reject) → Sign-off/Intent (verifyIntent, REQ-091) → Campaign Integration (assemble the LevelRecord-ready PipelineProduct + provenance). Emits an accepted product or a typed PipelineRejection (phase+reason+findings); deletion = no product persisted. Pure fold, deterministic under fixed seeds; the IRD P7 exit condition (accept end-to-end AND reject poisoned concepts at the correct phase). Public seams only (dm-0057).

S8.1 -- REQ-130 P8 share (dm-0065): the editor draft-authoring state model. LevelDraft mirrors the raw JSON shape parseLevel accepts, using plain string ids (EntityId/TriggerId brands are minted only by the schema validator, dm-0013) plus editor-only groups metadata (no schema representation, dropped at export). applyCommand/undo/redo are FULL-SNAPSHOT based (not hand-rolled inverses) so undo restores the exact prior draft by construction. Commands: paintTile, placeEntity (grid-snapped via snapToGrid), groupEntities, ungroup. exportDraft is a thin call into the real parseLevel -- zero new validation logic. Pure logic/data only; no rendering (dm-0065 -- presentation is P9).

S8.1 -- REQ-130 P8 share (dm-0065): the live in-editor playtest driver. startPlaytest(def, seed) assembles the EXISTING Engine/StateManager with CANONICAL_PIPELINE (src/eval/AgentHarness -- "the only sanctioned engine assembly order"), not a new simulation. feedInput commits the frame as state.input then advances exactly one fixed step (engine.tick(FIXED_STEP_SECONDS)) -- the identical per-tick drive contract AgentHarness.runAgent/replayTape already use, proven by test to reproduce replayTape bit-for-bit given the same frames. stepFrame = feedInput(NEUTRAL_INPUT) (frame-stepping substrate for S8.3). commit exposes the one sanctioned mutation point (dm-0070) for the S8.3 Inspector. reload re-instantiates via createInitialState. Pure interactive wrapper; no rendering (dm-0065).

S8.2 -- REQ-131 P8 share part 1 (dm-0065): pure debug-overlay descriptors, all plain data (no rendering). hitboxDescriptors: player (TUNING.playerHalfExtents) + every entity AABB at position+collider.offset. triggerDescriptors: each wiring records source/target positions resolved by entity id. pathDescriptors: a movers OWN AUTHORED waypoints offset by its transform position -- static level data, zero duplication of EntityKinematics closed-form math (dm-0016), which resolves WHERE a mover is, not the paths shape. jumpArcDescriptor: folds the REAL playerControlSystem+stepPlayerPhysics (P3, unmodified) over a local state copy for N steps, never committing -- a read-only forward preview, test-proven not to mutate the live session. normalDescriptor/physicsStateDescriptor: read existing WorldState fields only (dm-0009); groundNormal is (0,-1) y-down iff grounded, else honestly null (no wall-contact side is tracked).

S8.3 -- REQ-131 P8 share part 2 (dm-0070): runtime inspection controller wrapping an S8.1 PlaytestSession. pause/resume gate feedInput (a no-op while paused); stepFrame always advances exactly one tick with neutral input regardless of pause state (frame-step-while-paused is the normal debugging motion). setVariable(patch) is the load-bearing decision: a debug variable edit is NOT an in-place snapshot mutation (would violate dm-0004/dm-0043) -- it builds an explicit next state {...current, world:{...current.world, ...patch}} and commits through the SAME StateManager.commit() every system output uses (exposed by PlaytestSession.commit), the one sanctioned mutation point, test-proven to leave the prior frozen snapshot untouched and to drive the engine deterministically on the next step. reload re-instantiates via the session. paused is local controller bookkeeping, not game state. No rendering (dm-0065).

S8.4 -- REQ-132 P8 share (dm-0067): profiling instrumentation. Profiler.timeSection(label, fn) wraps a synchronous section, accumulates count/total/mean/max under the label, returns the callees value unchanged; sceneLoadTiming times a loader callback (no import of instantiateWorld -- caller supplies it); countAllocations is a manual deterministic tally (NOT process.memoryUsage, which is non-reproducible); report() gives a read-only ProfileReport sorted by label. THE ONE place permitted to read a wall clock (default performance.now) per dm-0067 -- diagnostic-only metadata that never feeds WorldState/replay and is never imported by src/; the clock is injectable so accumulation math is deterministically testable. Imports nothing from src/. No rendering (dm-0065).

S8.5 -- REQ-133 part 1 (dm-0068): live telemetry capture. recordSession(def, seed) reuses the S8.1 PlaytestSession driver (no independent capture loop); capture(frame) = session.feedInput with the frame appended; finalize() freezes the minimal replayable unit TelemetryRecord {levelId, seed, tape, outcome, attempts, ticksElapsed} -- every ArchetypeRun field except the archetype label (a live human capture has no archetype; S8.6 supplies the label to adapt it for analyzeTape, dm-0044). The normalized uint32 seed is read from the session initial rng.seed (= createRng(seed).seed), the canonical tape seed that re-normalizes idempotently, so replayTape of the finalized tape reproduces the run bit-for-bit with zero duplication of AgentHarness private normalizeSeed. Death coords/behavior signals are RECOVERED by replay in S8.6, never logged live (dm-0068). No eval internals, no gen, no rendering, no wall clock.

S8.6 -- FOURTH versioned strict-parsed calibration schema (dm-0073) alongside ScoringProfile/CampaignProfile/GenProfile. Holds heatmap.binWorldSize (>0). parseTelemetryProfile never throws, rejects unknown keys/bad version/non-positive bin. DEFAULT frozen (binWorldSize 1). Separate per dm-0073/dm-0057: telemetry calibration is P8-owned; coupling to CampaignProfile would force version bumps on an unchanged verified schema. Imports only the SchemaError type.

S8.6 -- REQ-133 part 2 + REQ-032 P8 share (dm-0068/dm-0069/dm-0073/dm-0074). deriveDeathHeatmap RECOVERS death coords by replaying each record via the verified replayObserved (AgentHarness seam) and binning defeated-tick positions by floor(pos/binWorldSize) -- no live position log (dm-0068). toArchetypeRun is the dm-0044 substitution point: adapts a live TelemetryRecord into the ArchetypeRun analyzeTape/processCampaign consume, supplying only the archetype label; no new spike-detection logic (stays P6 processCampaign, dm-0069). Imports gdos/Evidence ArchetypeRun + eval/Archetypes ArchetypeName as TYPES only (dm-0074). Test-proven heatmap==direct-replay; telemetry LevelRecord round-trips UNMODIFIED processCampaign (IRD P8 exit second half); zero campaign/ changes.

S9.1 -- the sixth versioned schema (dm-0083): the style-agnostic core of REQ-070/071. Six GrammarCategory records (safe/danger/interactive/temporary/optimization/secret), each with critical: boolean (danger hard-pinned true, REQ-016) and a human meaning string. Total, single-valued bindings map every RenderableRole (the closed EntityKind union from components/Behavior + terrain + goal) to exactly one category -- the structural half of REQ-070's mixing prohibition (the pack-side half is StylePack.validatePack). DEFAULT_GRAMMAR binds goal -> interactive per dm-0085 (terracotta means exactly "kills you") and movingPlatform/conveyor -> optimization per dm-0084 (achromatic motion glyphs). Strict parser: dm-0010/0014 discipline, never throws, unknown keys rejected, version hard-pinned. Contains ZERO colour/shape/sound -- a material in this file is a defect (dm-0083/dm-0076 swappability).

S9.1 -- the art-style-agnostic asset-provider seam (dm-0076/dm-0083). StylePack interface: visual(request) -> CachedVisual (opaque BitmapHandle + anchor + pad, cache-by-key per bible §5), paletteAccent/motionSpec/audioPatch per GrammarCategoryId. validatePack(pack, grammar) enforces the pack-side half of REQ-070's mixing prohibition: accent uniqueness across categories, danger MUST be chromatic (REQ-016), optimization MUST be achromatic (dm-0084, palette signature is ink/cream motion glyphs), motion-class uniqueness (bible §1's six classes), audio-patch pairwise distinctness (REQ-071), and a cache-by-key probe (same request -> same bitmap handle, "generate never per-frame"). SynthPatch (procedural audio, zero binary assets in P9) and MotionSpec are pure data compiled by later slices (S9.6 audio executor, S9.3 scene compiler). "Paper Collage" (render/style/paper/, S9.2) is StylePack #1; a future pack implements the same interface with zero gameplay/data changes. AMENDED at S9.2 (dm-0090): added rasterize(request, device: Raster2D): void — the actual draw-command seam (visual() only manages cache identity) — plus a determinism probe in validatePack (two trace recorders, byte-identical output required). render/style/Raster2D.ts (the enumerated 2D-drawing device interface + createTraceRecorder) is a new sibling dependency.

S9.2 -- the minimal enumerated 2D-drawing device seam (dm-0086). Raster2D interface: methods only (setFillStyle/setStrokeStyle/etc, never property assignment) so a recording fake can log every call without a Proxy. createTraceRecorder() returns a pure in-memory recording implementation used by StylePack.validatePack's determinism probe and by every render/style/paper/ structural test -- never touches a real canvas. render/platform/ (S9.8, not yet built) will be the ONLY place binding this to a real CanvasRenderingContext2D/OffscreenCanvasRenderingContext2D.

nodes.file_path: render/style/paper/*.ts (PaperRng, Geometry, Palette, PaperBlocks, PaperTiles, KindDraw, PaperStylePack)
nodes.functional_summary: S9.2 -- StylePack #1, "Paper Collage" (dm-0075), realizing render/style/StylePack.ts. Direct TypeScript port of src/assets/paper-asset-library.html's core generators (PaperRng: createRng/hashSeed; Geometry: roughPoly/roughRect/fillRough/wear/grain/roughLine/inkSplat; PaperBlocks: the 9-slice blockSlab library, dm-0079/dm-0080 clean-solid, no interior wear/grain; PaperTiles: the modular autotile kit, maskAt/paintTile, out-of-bounds-solid convention preserved; KindDraw: all 12 EntityKind base+overlay pairs, base seeded from KIND ALONE per dm-0078, pinned by a byte-identical-base-across-states regression test; drawPlayer/drawFlag ported, drawFlag recolored to GOAL_ACCENT=teal per dm-0085). dm-0091 corrects dm-0084's planning-time claim: the reference kit's Optimization elements were ALREADY achromatic, no recolor needed. dm-0092 clarifies identitySeed is deliberately ignored for all current roles (cache key role:state:T alone) -- entities/terrain/player/goal are vocabulary (bible principle 8), not per-instance-unique decor. PAPER_STYLE_PACK (the default instance) passes validatePack against DEFAULT_GRAMMAR cleanly. 12 structural tests (PaperStylePack.test.ts) -- no pixel inspection, command-trace equality only. BUG FIX at S9.5 (dm-0098): stateMatters() incorrectly excluded the player role from state-sensitive caching (grouped with goal, which genuinely IS state-invariant), collapsing idle/prejump/air into one cached bitmap and silently defeating REQ-150's anticipation-frame requirement. Fixed; player pose now caches correctly. Caught by GameFeel.test.ts's end-to-end integration test, not a unit test in isolation.

S9.3 -- render-layer-only viewpoint state (REQ-150 camera share). CameraState {x,y} never lives in WorldState/GameState; updateCamera(camera,target,profile) is a pure exponential-smoothing step toward a world position, threaded frame-to-frame by the shell (S9.8) exactly like StateManager threads sim state, but through its own separate chain. Never mutates its inputs (dm-0004/dm-0082 projection purity).

S9.3 -- the pure projection compileScene(current,previous,alpha,grammar,pack,camera,viewport,tileSizePx) -> DrawList (plain, JSON-serializable data). Interpolates entity/player positions between snapshots by alpha (interpolationAlpha stays read-only); resolves every visible role through render/grammar/Grammar.ts to exactly one category (player is the reserved null-category, always-critical case, dm-0004/dm-0082 extended). Culls entities via the P3 SpatialPartition quadtree READ-ONLY (buildEntityQuadtree/queryQuadtree, REQ-162's own wording -- reuse, not reimplementation); terrain culls by direct tile-range clamp; the goal by direct AABB check. Per-entity overlay state (laser on/off, door open/closed, collapsingFloor intact/cracking, gravityZone normal/invert) is derived ONLY from fields WorldState/LevelDefinition already expose -- never by re-deriving Sensors/trigger logic (dm-0093 notes pressurePlate/spring have no live signal yet and render idle/default). dm-0094 notes culling queries the quadtree against CURRENT (pre-interpolation) positions, a documented simplification. 7 tests (SceneCompiler.test.ts): interpolation at alpha in {0,0.5,1}, grammar resolution, critical-survives-culling, state derivation, goal AABB culling, JSON round-trip purity, WorldState-unchanged proof. AMENDED at S9.4 (dm-0095): DrawItem gained a `request: VisualRequest` field so a downstream consumer (the atlas) can rasterize a bitmap id it has never seen before.

S9.4 -- the WebGL2 executor pipeline (REQ-161 pooling elsewhere; REQ-162 batching; REQ-170 WebGL share). Gl2Device: minimal enumerated WebGL2RenderingContext subset (createTexture/uploadTextureRegion/createBuffer/uploadBufferData/drawInstanced) + createTraceGl2Device, a pure in-memory recording implementation for every render/gl/ test -- never a real GPU. Atlas: shelf-packs cached StylePack bitmaps into fixed-size pages (AtlasProfile.pageSizePx, data not a literal), uploading each distinct bitmap id EXACTLY ONCE via an injected producePixels thunk (real rasterization is render/platform/'s job, S9.8; headless tests use fakePixelData). Batcher: groups a DrawList into one Batch per atlas page -- dozens of identical terrain-tile bitmaps collapse into ONE instanced draw call, the actual REQ-162 batching win (dm-0096: this is instanced same-page batching, NOT a merged "one background bitmap", which remains explicitly deferred). GlRenderer: packs per-instance transforms (world position + atlas UV rect) into a Float32Array and replays exactly one uploadBufferData+drawInstanced pair per batch. 8 tests (GlExecutor.test.ts): upload-once semantics, page-bounds/no-overlap, batching draw-call-count reduction, instance-float correctness, defensive no-texture skip, custom page-size honored.

S9.4 -- generic fixed-capacity, generation-counted object pool (REQ-161: particles, visual impacts, projectiles). acquire() recycles the oldest-freed slot (FIFO free-queue) or returns null on exhaustion (never silently evicts an active instance); release() is a safe no-op on a stale or already-released handle (generation-mismatch guard, tested for double-release). ParticlePool is the REQ-161 named instantiation; capacity comes from ParticlePoolProfile data (DEFAULT_PARTICLE_POOL_PROFILE), never a literal. Consumed by S9.5's game-feel modifiers. 6 tests (Pool.test.ts).

S9.5 -- game-feel modifiers (REQ-150 visual sub-clauses) + the fairness defeat marker (REQ-016). FeelProfile: versioned render-tuning data (squash/stretch curve, jump/landing burst counts/speed/lifetime) -- zero literals in the logic modules. SquashStretch: deriveSquashStretch(velocityY, profile) -> Scale, a pure velocity-driven curve, identity at rest, sign-independent, clamped at velocityForMaxEffect (pinned by monotonicity + clamp + fixed-point tests). ImpulseEvents: jumpFired/landed are pure edge detectors over two WorldState snapshots (anticipating->spent; airborne->grounded) -- never a continuous condition, so a burst fires exactly once; spawnBurst acquires up to profile.count particles from the S9.4 ParticlePool using an INJECTED seeded RngFn (never Math.random, never the sim's own Rng stream), returning fewer handles gracefully on near-exhaustion. DefeatMarker: deriveDefeatMarker(world) resolves dm-0089 (S9.1's open question) -- null unless runState==='defeated'; when present, always {worldX,worldY,critical:true} at the player position. dm-0097 records the resolution: the marker's screen time comes from the existing fixed-step/render decoupling (the shell renders every real frame against the same committed defeated snapshot before the next tick reloads), not a new hold mechanism or a Lifecycle.ts change. Also caught and fixed a real S9.2 bug (dm-0098): stateMatters() wrongly treated player as state-invariant, collapsing all three poses onto one cached bitmap. 15 tests (GameFeel.test.ts), including an end-to-end anticipation-frame proof through the real SceneCompiler+PaperStylePack pipeline.

S9.6 -- WebAudio procedural signatures + cue derivation (REQ-071 audio share; REQ-170 WebAudio share). AudioDevice: minimal enumerated AudioContext/OscillatorNode/GainNode subset + createTraceAudioDevice, a pure recorder, no real audio graph. PatchCompiler.compilePatch(patch, device) turns a category SynthPatch (PaperStylePack.AUDIO_PATCH, S9.2) into a gain-envelope + oscillator-or-noise-source + master-connect call sequence; noise-waveform patches never touch createOscillator/setFrequencyRamp and vice versa. CueDerivation.deriveAudioCues(previous, current, grammar) is pure and transition-driven, reusing render/feel/ImpulseEvents.landed (S9.5) rather than re-deriving the airborne->grounded edge; emits exactly three cue shapes (landed categorized by what was landed on, defeat->danger, goal->interactive) -- dm-0099 documents why a generic jump-liftoff cue and a checkpoint cue are deliberately NOT built (no bound category for the former; the mechanic does not exist for the latter, grep-verified). AudioExecutor gates scheduleCues on a mute flag (dm-0100): muteForBreak() stops ANY device calls, not just silences them, matching the Poki ad-break requirement's spirit, not just its letter. 11 tests (Audio.test.ts).

S9.7 -- asset manifest + async delivery + delivery-speed profiling (REQ-163 async share; REQ-132 P9 share, dm-0072; REQ-002 no-long-loads). AssetManifest is the SEVENTH versioned schema (strict-parsed, dm-0010/0014 discipline): entries with id/url/kind/priority(critical|deferred)/bytes. AssetFetcher + WallClock are injected seams (dm-0086) -- no real fetch()/Date.now() outside render/platform/. AssetLoader splits delivery into loadCriticalTier (blocks first render on every critical asset; a failure halts and surfaces a typed {id,message} error, never throws) and streamDeferredTier (runs after, degrading -- skipping, recording ok:false -- a failed deferred asset without blocking or throwing); totalManifestBytes is the progress denominator. Per-asset elapsedMs (via the injected WallClock) is diagnostic-only data, the same confinement contract as dm-0067/dm-0082. dm-0101 fixed a real RenderIsolation false-positive this slice surfaced: the scan's blanket fetch( pattern flagged AssetFetcher's legitimate interface method; narrowed to catch only a real bare global call. 22 tests (AssetManifest.test.ts + AssetLoader.test.ts).

S9.8 -- the per-frame orchestrator (REQ-170/171/001/002). GameShell.createGameShell wraps Engine+StateManager+CANONICAL_PIPELINE (the same P8 Playtest.ts precedent, extended with real-time-driven step counts via Engine.tick(realDeltaSeconds), camera smoothing, compileScene, audio-cue derivation, the defeat marker, and Poki lifecycle triggers) -- projection-purity PROVEN: sim state bit-identical to a headless Engine/StateManager/CANONICAL_PIPELINE run fed the same InputFrames. gameplayStart fires on first meaningful input, gameplayStop on leaving 'playing', both re-arming across a reload (proven against a REAL spike-defeat scenario, not a fabricated WorldState). ViewportModel.computeViewport is pure letterbox/DPR-cap[1,2]/fullscreen-passthrough math (REQ-171). InputCapture is pure key-state->InputFrame mapping with edge-triggered jump/reset (matching InputFrame's own contract) + shouldPreventDefault for space/arrows (REQ-171 no-page-scroll). PortalSdk is the typed Poki lifecycle interface + NullPortalSdk (dev/test AND Poki's own recommended local-dev mode). PortalLifecycle orchestrates boot->reportLoadingFinished ordering and wraps commercialBreak with mute+input-suspend (via/finally, restores even on rejection). dm-0102/dm-0103 fixed RenderIsolation false-positives found this slice (devicePixelRatio field name; fetch already fixed at S9.7). dm-0104 (see below) explicitly defers render/platform/ -- the real browser-global bindings (canvas/rAF/AudioContext/PokiSDK CDN adapter) -- to when a real browser/build pipeline exists (P11); no stub files created. 24 tests (GameShell.test.ts + ViewportModel.test.ts + InputCapture.test.ts + PortalLifecycle.test.ts).

S9.9 -- REQ-130/131 P9 render/UI share: the ONLY render/ area permitted to import tools/ (dm-0081). Overlay.ts.paintOverlays(state, jumpArcTicks) composes all six P8 tools/debug/Overlay.ts descriptor kinds (hitbox/trigger/path/jumpArc/normal/physicsState) verbatim into a semantic, device-free OverlayDrawList (rect/polyline/label primitives) -- colour/stroke choices deliberately deferred to a future render/platform/ executor (P11), same precedent as dm-0103. EditorSurface.ts.compileEditorScene(draft, grammar, pack, tileSizePx, selection) paints a P8 LevelDraft (tools/level_editor/EditorState.ts) through the REAL StylePack/Grammar pipeline for terrain+entities (reusing render/scene/SceneCompiler.ts's isSolidTile/terrainMask/terrainVariant/visualItem verbatim, amended this slice to be tilemap-scoped rather than WorldState-scoped so a LevelDraft.tilemap -- structurally identical to WorldState.level.tilemap -- can reuse them with zero duplicated autotile logic) -- WYSIWYG: the editor draws exactly what the shipped scene compiler would. grid/selection are editor-only UI chrome (no grammar signature) as a separate StylePack-free primitive list. mapEditorInput(event, draft) maps raw pointer/key events to P8's EditorCommand union or undo/redo, never applying it -- P8's applyCommand/undo/redo/snapToGrid are called by the TEST, never re-implemented here (proven: placeEntity through this mapping still lands grid-snapped via P8's own logic). InspectorBindings.ts.applyInspectorCommand dispatches a closed UI-command union to exactly one tools/debug/Inspector.ts method call each -- zero new mutation logic, dm-0070's commit-through-StateManager discipline stays entirely inside Inspector.setVariable. Zero P8 module modified. 18 new tests (ToolingOverlay.test.ts, EditorSurface.test.ts, InspectorBindings.test.ts).

S9.10 -- REQ-163 dynamic quality scale-back, the IRD P9 exit condition. QualityProfile.ts: render-layer tuning data (dm-0083, same class as CameraProfile/FeelProfile, not a full versioned external schema) -- an ordered best-to-worst tier ladder (full/reducedParticles/noDeferredDecoration/noGrain), each rung carrying a nonCriticalBudget (null only on the best tier), emaAlpha, degrade/upgradeThresholdMs (hysteresis gap), cooldownTicks, stabilityWindowTicks. QualityController.ts: createQualityController/currentTier/reportFrameTime -- a pure frame-time EMA state machine (QualityControllerState is render-layer-only, never WorldState/GameState, threaded exactly like CameraState per dm-0004/dm-0082); degrade takes priority over upgrade on any tick where both would fire, steps exactly one rung, never skips; upgrade additionally requires stabilityWindowTicks CONSECUTIVE good ticks (any bad tick resets the streak to zero); both directions share one cooldownTicks floor since the last transition, which is what actually prevents boundary oscillation (hysteresis alone is not sufficient at tick-by-tick cadence). QualityTier.ts: applyTier(drawList, tier) -- the REQ-016 x REQ-163 interlock itself, already pinned as a contract comment on DrawItem.critical since S9.3/S9.4: order-preserving, keeps every critical item unconditionally and truncates critical:false items to the tier budget. Zero-assumption finding: particles live entirely in ParticlePool (never enter DrawList) and grain/decor are baked into PaperStylePack.rasterize() bitmap content -- neither is a separate DrawItem field, so the tier ladder gates via nonCriticalBudget truncation over DrawList rather than a per-item decor/particle/grain classification that does not exist in the shipped code (dm-0107). 12 new tests (QualityController.test.ts) -- the IRD exit condition: hysteresis gap, cooldown-blocks-oscillation, stability-window-must-be-consecutive, never-drops-critical, tier-index bounds, purity.
